title: Run Full AD Enumeration (SharpHound)
description: Executes the SharpHound ingestor using the 'All' collection method. It gathers users, groups, sessions, trusts, and ACLs across the entire domain and packages them into a ZIP file for offline analysis in the BloodHound GUI.
when_to_use: Your primary command for mapping an Active Directory environment once you have compromised a domain-joined machine or have valid domain credentials.
common_pitfalls: The 'All' collection method is extremely noisy and can easily trigger modern EDR alerts due to the massive volume of LDAP queries and remote registry connections.

title: Extract All Domain Users via LDAP
description: Connects to the Domain Controller via LDAP using valid credentials to dump a list of all user accounts (specifically extracting the sAMAccountName attribute) in the domain.
when_to_use: When you are attacking from a Linux machine and have obtained low-privileged AD credentials, and you need a valid list of usernames for password spraying or AS-REP Roasting.

title: List Domain Admins
description: Queries the Domain Controller to list the direct members of the highly privileged 'Domain Admins' group.
when_to_use: Use this to identify your ultimate high-value targets within the network. These are the users you want to avoid attacking directly, but whose sessions you want to steal.
common_pitfalls: This only shows direct members. Nested groups (e.g., an 'IT_Admins' group placed inside 'Domain Admins') will not be recursively expanded by this command.
